title: Prepare to Install on iSeries
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
text: Prepare to Install on iSeries You can install Uniface in one of two ways—using a virtual optical drive, or using CL commands to restore and run the installer. Using a Virtual Optical Drive If you have a virtual optical drive that was created using CRTDEVOPT : Create an image catalog for it using WRKIMGCLG . Add a new entry for the Uniface electronic distribution to the image catalog using WRKIMGCLG option 12. This new entry must have an image size of 900 MB or more. Mount the new entry and load the image catalog. Initialize the entry using WRKIMGCLGE option 10 and give the Volume Identifier AS400_UF (this name is required). When that has finished, your new empty virtual DVD will be ready under /QOPT/AS400_UF in the IFS. For more information about creating and using virtual optical devices and image catalogs, refer to the IBM Knowledge Center or other IBM i documentation. To extract the distribution onto your virtual DVD: Use IBM Navigator for i to create a File Share to /QOPT with Read/Write access. Map this new share as a drive to your Windows workstation. We will assume this drive is U: . Now run the self-extracting executable. Click Browse , browse to U:/AS400_UF and click Extract . The Uniface electronic distribution will now be extracted onto your virtual optical drive. Using CL Commands to Restore the Installer You need to extract the contents of the Uniface self-extracting executable to an IFS (Integrated File System) share on iSeries and restore the installer. Note: Installing from an NFS-mounted drive is not recommended, and is not supported. The Uniface installer uses the CPY CL command. This command is known to have problems copying from NFS-mounted directories to the local system. The installer might fail to run, or might fail to copy some or all files when the Uniface distribution resides on an NFS-mounted directory. It is recommended that you extract the distribution into an empty directory that resides in the / Root file system or in the /QopenSys file system. To extract the distribution: Use IBM Navigator for i to create a File Share to your chosen directory on the iSeries, or use an existing share that has Read/Write access. Map this share as a drive to your Windows workstation. We will assume this drive is U: . Run the self-extracting zip file on your Windows workstation and click Browse . Navigate to the U: drive, and possibly to a sub-directory on that drive, until you reach the destination IFS directory. Now click Extract to extract the Uniface distribution into the iSeries IFS. Make a note of the exact location in the IFS into which you have extracted the Uniface distribution; you will need it to restore and run the Uniface installer. In what follows we will assume this IFS directory is /uniface . Note: It is recommended that you restore the installer into library QTEMP because that library is automatically cleaned up when you sign off. To restore the installer into library QTEMP : Copy the savefile from the extracted distribution into library QTEMP . The savefile is located in a directory with the same name as the installation code for your version of IBM i, for example AS3 or AS4 . Run the following CL command to copy the IFS version of the savefile into a real savefile in library QTEMP : CPYFRMSTMF FROMSTMF('/uniface/AS3/AS400INS.SVF') TOMBR('/QSYS.LIB/QTEMP.LIB/INSTALL.FILE') MBROPT(*REPLACE) Copy This example assumes that you have extracted the distribution into the IFS directory /uniface and that your installation code is AS3 If you extracted to a different directory, replace /uniface in the CL commands with the path you have extracted to, and if your installation code is not AS3 replace AS3 in the path with the actual installation code. Restore the AS400INS program and its display file into library QTEMP : RSTOBJ OBJ(*ALL) SAVLIB(UNIFLIB) DEV(*SAVF) OBJTYPE(*ALL) SAVF(QTEMP/INSTALL) RSTLIB(QTEMP) Copy This extracts the installer program and display files from the savefile into library QTEMP. Restore the installer. You can now run the installer to install Uniface. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
